## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Use this server whenever the user wants to create, generate, edit, implement, or sync any design, UI, screen, component, mockup, or visual — in Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or Figma Slides — and whenever the user mentions Figma or provides a figma.com URL.

This server bridges code and design in both directions, and supports designing from scratch using existing design systems and codebases.

CAPABILITIES:
- Read designs FROM Figma into code (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Write designs INTO Figma from code, intent, or existing components (use_figma, generate_figma_design, create_new_file, upload_assets)
- Bridge code and design via Code Connect (get_code_connect_map, add_code_connect_map)
- Create diagrams and FigJam content (generate_diagram, get_figjam)

WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user wants to create, mock up, or generate any UI, screen, component, or design — even if Figma isn't named
- The user wants to implement a design as code (design-to-code)
- The user wants to push a page, view, or component into Figma (code-to-design)
- The user wants to update, sync, or edit an existing Figma file
- The user shares a figma.com URL
- The user wants to build or extend a design system, design tokens, or component library
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

SKILLS (Prefer the skills shipped with the Figma plugin on the agent being used. If no Figma plugin is installed, use the skills served by the Figma MCP resource):
- /figma-use — MANDATORY before calling use_figma; fallback: skill://figma/figma-use/SKILL.md
- /figma-generate-design — for translating an app page or layout into Figma; fallback: skill://figma/figma-generate-design/SKILL.md
- /figma-generate-library — for building a design system in Figma from code; fallback: skill://figma/figma-generate-library/SKILL.md
- /figma-code-connect — for mapping Figma components to codebase components; fallback: skill://figma/figma-code-connect/SKILL.md
- /figma-use-figjam — for FigJam-specific use_f… [truncated]

## claude.ai tldraw
Use `search` to query the tldraw Editor API spec (e.g. search for methods by category or name). Use `exec` to run JavaScript on the canvas — your code receives `editor` (the tldraw Editor instance) and helpers like toRichText, createShapeId, createArrowBetweenShapes. The current canvas state is kept in model context as raw TLShape, asset, and binding data.

## pencil
The Pencil MCP server is the editor for .pen design files (web/mobile apps and websites). Use it to read, generate, and validate designs.

.pen files are encrypted: access them only via "pencil" MCP tools — never use Read or Grep on .pen files. Follow each tool's input schema exactly.

Tools: get_editor_state, open_document, get_guidelines, batch_get, batch_design, snapshot_layout, get_screenshot, get_variables, set_variables, find_empty_space_on_canvas, search_all_unique_properties, replace_all_matching_properties, export_nodes.

## plugin:personal-os:notebooklm-mcp
NotebookLM MCP - Access NotebookLM (notebooklm.google.com).

**Auth:** If you get authentication errors, run `nlm login` via your Bash/terminal tool. This is the automated authentication method that handles everything. Only use save_auth_tokens as a fallback if the CLI fails.
**Account Switching:** To switch Google Accounts for the MCP server, run `nlm login switch <profile>` in Bash. The MCP server instantly uses the active default profile.
**Confirmation:** Tools with confirm param require user approval before setting confirm=True.
**Studio:** After creating audio/video/infographic/slides, poll studio_status for completion.

Consolidated tools:
- source_add(source_type=url|text|drive|file, url=..., document_id=..., text=..., file_path=...): Add any source type
- studio_create(artifact_type=audio|video|...): Create any artifact type
- studio_revise: Revise individual slides in an existing slide deck
- download_artifact(artifact_type=audio|video|...): Download any artifact type
- note_create/note_list/note_update/note_delete: Manage notes in notebooks

## redact-mcp
REDACTION MODE IS ACTIVE. You MUST follow these rules for the ENTIRE session:

1. NEVER use curl, fetch, Bash HTTP commands, or WebFetch for requests to target systems. ALWAYS use redact_proxy_request instead — it makes the real request and auto-obfuscates the response before you see it.

2. NEVER use the Read tool for client data files, configs, or logs. ALWAYS use redact_read_file instead — it auto-obfuscates file contents.

3. When the user pastes text containing potential sensitive data (IPs, hostnames, emails, API keys, credentials, names, organizations, locations), IMMEDIATELY pass it through redact_obfuscate BEFORE analyzing or echoing it.

4. NEVER output real/unredacted sensitive values in your responses. You should only ever see and work with the obfuscated replacements (e.g., 198.51.100.x, target-N.example.com, user-N@example.com, [REDACTED_KEY_N]).

5. Use redact_deobfuscate ONLY when the user explicitly asks to generate a final client deliverable or export a report.

6. These rules apply automatically. No user configuration is needed. Do not ask the user whether to redact — just do it.

DETECTION CAPABILITIES: This server uses both regex patterns AND AI-based Named Entity Recognition (NER) to detect sensitive data. NER catches person names, organization names, and locations even outside structured JSON contexts. Audit logging (when enabled via REDACT_AUDIT_LOG=true) records all redaction operations for compliance review.

1	# VietNexus — Architecture
2	
3	How the system is put together: services, request flow, data model, and deployment.
4	For the *why* behind each technology choice, see [`TECHNOLOGY.md`](TECHNOLOGY.md).
5	
6	---
7	
8	## 1. System at a glance
9	
10	```
11	                         ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
12	   Web (Vite/React) ─┐   │                 VPS nginx                  │
13	                     ├──▶│  TLS termination · path routing · tunnels  │
14	   Mobile (Expo)  ───┘   │           https://dqplus.ddns.net          │
15	                         └───────────────────────────────────────────┘
16	                                            │  /api/*
17	             ┌──────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┐
18	             ▼                               ▼                               ▼
19	     ┌───────────────┐             ┌───────────────────┐          ┌───────────────────┐
20	     │   Gateway     │             │  Extract Agent    │          │  Matching Engine  │
21	     │  :3000        │             │  :3001 (:3003 loc)│          │  :3002            │
22	     │  auth+profiles│             │  attrs+embeddings │          │  vector+attr rank │
23	     └──────┬────────┘             └─────────┬─────────┘          └─────────┬─────────┘
24	            │  profiles                       │ extracted_profiles          │ reads
25	            ▼                                 ▼                             ▼
26	     ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
27	     │           Postgres 16 + pgvector  (DB: dealflow · shared by all services)        │
28	     └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
29	                                            ▲
30	                                            │  same DB
31	                    ┌───────────────────────┴────────────────────────┐
32	                    │   AI Data Platform (Python spine + agents)      │
33	                    │   FastAPI read API :8000 · supervisor · sagas   │
34	                    └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
35	```
36	
37	Three Python/FastAPI services own the app's live path; a Python spine (the AI Data Platform)
38	shares the same Postgres for research-grade, evidence-cited matchmaking. `nginx` on the VPS
39	is the single public entry point and routes by URL prefix.
40	
41	## 2. Services
42	
43	### 2.1 Gateway — `backend/gateway` (:3000)
44	
45	The front door for identity and profile data.
46	
47	- **Auth**: `POST /auth/register`, `POST /auth/login` (bcrypt + JWT), `GET /auth/me`.
48	- **Profiles** (JWT-protected): `POST /profiles`, `GET /profiles/:id`, `PATCH /profiles/:id`.
49	- **Health**: `GET /health` (checks DB connectivity).
50	- DB access: **asyncpg** with raw SQL; an idempotent DDL bootstrap at startup keeps the
51	  schema aligned (and widens `users.role` with `admin`). Swagger docs are FastAPI-native
52	  at `/docs`.
53	- Owns the `users` and `profiles` tables.
54	
55	### 2.2 Extract Agent — `backend/agent/extract` (:3001 canonical, **:3003 on this machine**)
56	
57	Turns a profile (or raw text / a crawled page) into a canonical attribute set plus an
58	embedding, and writes one row per user into `extracted_profiles`.
59	
60	- `POST /extract/profile` — load a gateway profile by `userId`, map it to canonical
61	  attributes, run the pipeline, upsert the row. (This is what the app calls.)
62	- `POST /extract/text`, `POST /extract/crawl` — same pipeline from raw text / a URL.
63	- `GET /extracted/:userId` — fetch the stored extraction.
64	- **Extraction** uses an OpenAI-compatible chat model with a strict JSON-schema response
65	  (`founder.schema.js` / `investor.schema.js`) against the FPT-cloud endpoint.
66	- **Embedding** uses `text-embedding-3-small` (1536-dim). If `OPENAI_API_KEY` is unset, it
67	  falls back to a deterministic **feature-hash embedding** (L2-normalized bag-of-words) so
68	  cosine ranking still works on lexical overlap — the pipeline never hard-fails.
69	- On boot it self-initializes its schema (`db/init.js` → `schema.sql`): creates the
70	  `vector` extension, the `extracted_profiles` table, and an **HNSW** cosine index.
71	
72	> **Port note:** `.env.example` pins `PORT=3001`, but on the dev machine Uptime Kuma
73	> occupies 3001, so it runs on **3003** locally. The vite proxy and nginx tunnels are
74	> configured for 3003 accordingly.
75	
76	### 2.3 Matching Engine — `backend/matching-engine` (:3002)
77	
78	Ranks the opposite side of the market for a given user.
79	
80	- `GET /matches/founders/:userId/investors` and
81	  `GET /matches/investors/:userId/founders` — the app's two live routes. Return `404` if
82	  the user has no `extracted_profiles` row yet (the app then triggers extraction and
83	  retries).
84	- `GET /matches` — **proxied** to the Python matching API (`MATCHING_API_URL`, default
85	  `:8000`) for the composite research-grade list.
86	- **Algorithm** (`matching.service.js` + `scoring.js`):
87	  1. Pull the requester's embedding, then fetch the `CANDIDATE_POOL` (default 50) nearest
88	     rows of the target role by pgvector cosine distance (`<=>`), with optional
89	     sector/stage/region pre-filters on the JSONB attributes.
90	  2. Compute `vectorScore = 1 − cosine_distance`.
91	  3. Compute `attributeScore` from explainable rules: **sector overlap** (Jaccard, ×0.4),
92	     **stage match** (+0.3), **geography match / global** (+0.2), **check size fits ask**
93	     (+0.05–0.1). Each firing rule adds a human-readable reason.
94	  4. Final `score = 0.7 · vectorScore + 0.3 · attributeScore` (weights configurable via
95	     `MATCH_VECTOR_WEIGHT` / `MATCH_ATTR_WEIGHT`), sorted descending, sliced to `limit`.
96	
97	### 2.4 AI Data Platform — `ai-data-platform/` (Python)
98	
99	A deterministic supervisor (event-sourced sagas, leased jobs, retries, dead-letter) that
100	drives isolated `feynman` / `pi` agent runtimes over stdio JSON-RPC for enrichment,
101	extraction, ranking, drafting, and verification — every fact cited, every draft
102	fact-checked. Exposes a FastAPI **read API + dashboard** on `:8000`
103	(`/entities /matches /sagas /jobs /events`). Full detail lives in its own docs:
104	[`ai-data-platform/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ai-data-platform/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) and
105	[`AGENT_FLOWS.md`](../ai-data-platform/docs/AGENT_FLOWS.md).
106	
107	## 3. Request flow — "show me my matches"
108	
109	```
110	1. User completes profile      → Gateway  POST /profiles            (row in `profiles`)
111	2. App requests matches        → Matching GET  /matches/.../investors
112	3.   no extraction yet? (404)  → Extract  POST /extract/profile     (row in `extracted_profiles`)
113	4. App retries matches         → Matching GET  /matches/.../investors → ranked list + reasons
114	5. User opens a match          → outreach draft generated (client-side template)
115	```
116	
117	The frontend encodes exactly this in `App.jsx` / `lib/api.js`: on a `404` from the matching
118	engine it auto-calls extraction, then retries the match request once.
119	
120	## 4. Data model
121	
122	All services share **one Postgres database (`dealflow`)**.
123	
124	**`users`** (gateway) — credentials + `role` (`founder` | `investor` | `admin`), links to a
125	profile. `admin` is never accepted by `/auth/register` — it is provisioned only by a direct
126	DB update (`UPDATE users SET role='admin' ...`) and unlocks the AI Data Platform dashboard.
127	
128	**`profiles`** (gateway) — the structured onboarding form: `company_name`,
129	`stage`, `industry`, `where_you_operate`, `website[]`, `description_product`, contact,
130	and investor economics (`avg_initial_investment`, `annual_investment_count`,
131	`avg_holding_period`, `year_founded`, `num_of_employees`, …). UUID PK, `underscored`,
132	timestamped.
133	
134	**`extracted_profiles`** (extract agent) — one row per user, the matching substrate:
135	
136	| Column | Purpose |
137	|--------|---------|
138	| `user_id` (UUID, unique) | FK-by-convention to the gateway user |
139	| `role` | `founder` \| `investor` |
140	| `source` | `profile` \| `text` \| `crawler` |
141	| `attributes` (JSONB) | canonical, queryable attribute set |
142	| `embedding_text` | the text that was embedded |
143	| `embedding` (`vector(1536)`) | semantic vector, **HNSW cosine index** |
144	
145	The AI Data Platform adds its own `core` / `orchestration` / `control_plane` schemas
146	(entities, sources with `source_url`, matches, sagas, jobs, events) via
147	`ai-data-platform/migrations/`.
148	
149	## 5. Frontend & mobile
150	
151	- **Web** (`frontend/`): Vite + React 18 + GSAP. No CORS on the backends — the dev/preview
152	  server and production nginx both **proxy `/api/*`** by prefix (`/api/backend` → gateway,
153	  `/api/agents` → extract, `/api/matches` → matching engine, `/api` → gateway fallback).
154	  Session (`vn.session`) and a per-user profile mirror live in `localStorage`.
155	- **Mobile** (`mobile/`): Expo / React Native (React Navigation, Google Fonts) mirroring
156	  the web screens (auth, profile form, matches, match detail). Currently backed by
157	  `src/data/mockData.js`.
158	
159	## 6. Deployment
160	
161	- **Public URL**: `https://dqplus.ddns.net`.
162	- **VPS** (`13.250.9.139`, Ubuntu): `nginx` terminates TLS and routes by prefix to
163	  **autossh reverse tunnels** from the dev machine (managed by `deploy/port-forward.sh`,
164	  targeting `127.0.0.1`):
165	  - `/` → `8443` → `proxy` container (`:5173`)
166	  - `/api/agents/`, `/api/extract/` → `5002` → extract agent (`:3003`)
167	  - `/api/matches/` → `5003` → matching engine (`:3002`)
168	  - `/api/backend/`, `/api/` fallback → `5000` → gateway (`:3000`)
169	- Production build uses `VITE_API_BASE=/api` (same-origin).
170	- **Local dev**: `./start.sh` boots Postgres (pgvector) via docker-compose, copies
171	  `.env.example → .env` where missing, and launches all three Node services with tailed
172	  logs into `logs/`.
173	
174	### 6.1 Zero-downtime web deploys
175	
176	The tunnel lands on a single local port (`:5173`), and only one container can bind a
177	host port — so a naive `docker compose up --build web` stops the old container before
178	the new one binds, dropping requests. To avoid that, the `web` service is **internal-only
179	and scalable** (no `container_name`, no published port, `expose: 80`, IPv4 healthcheck),
180	and a tiny **`proxy`** service (`nginx:1.27-alpine`, config in `deploy/proxy.conf`) owns
181	`:5173` and load-balances across `web` replicas:
182	
183	```
184	VPS nginx → :8443 → SSH tunnel → proxy (:5173) ─┬─ web (old replica)
185	                                                └─ web (new replica)
186	```
187	
188	The proxy resolves `web` per request via Docker DNS (`resolver 127.0.0.11`, variable
189	`proxy_pass`), so scaled replicas are discovered automatically, and `proxy_next_upstream`
190	with a short `proxy_connect_timeout` retries a sibling if one is draining.
191	
192	**Deploy a frontend change** with `bash deploy/rolling-deploy.sh`: build → start a 2nd
193	`web` replica → wait healthy → drain the old replica by container id → back to one. Always
194	≥1 healthy backend behind the proxy (verified `ok=800 fail=0` under an in-flight probe).
195	
196	**Operational notes**
197	
198	- `docker-compose.yml` pins `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` so the `vector` extension is durable
199	  across container recreation (an earlier apt-installed pgvector in a sibling container was
200	  ephemeral).
201	- autossh tunnels must use an **absolute** key path with `-f` and target `127.0.0.1`
202	  (WSL2 mirrored networking makes docker-published ports unreachable via the LAN IP). The
203	  VPS sshd rate-limits rapid reconnects (~1–2 min lockout).
204	- Container healthchecks hit `127.0.0.1`, not `localhost` — `localhost` resolves to `::1`
205	  and the services bind IPv4 only, which would report a healthy container as unhealthy.
206	- `docker compose --scale web=N` scales **down** by removing the newest replica, so the
207	  rolling script drains the *old* replica by id rather than scaling down.
208	- Change `deploy/proxy.conf` with `docker exec dqplus-proxy nginx -s reload` (zero
209	  downtime); recreating the `proxy` container briefly drops `:5173`.

             ▼                               ▼                               ▼
     ┌───────────────┐             ┌───────────────────┐          ┌───────────────────┐
     │   Gateway     │             │  Extract Agent    │          │  Matching Engine  │
     │  :3000        │             │  :3001 (:3003 loc)│          │  :3002            │
     │  auth+profiles│             │  attrs+embeddings │          │  vector+attr rank │
     └──────┬────────┘             └─────────┬─────────┘          └─────────┬─────────┘
            │  profiles                       │ extracted_profiles          │ reads
            ▼                                 ▼                             ▼
     ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
     │           Postgres 16 + pgvector  (DB: dealflow · shared by all services)        │
     └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                            ▲
                                            │  same DB
                    ┌───────────────────────┴────────────────────────┐
                    │   AI Data Platform (Python spine + agents)      │
                    │   FastAPI read API :8000 · supervisor · sagas   │
                    └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Three Python/FastAPI services own the app's live path; a Python spine (the AI Data Platform)
shares the same Postgres for research-grade, evidence-cited matchmaking. `nginx` on the VPS
is the single public entry point and routes by URL prefix.

## 2. Services

### 2.1 Gateway — `backend/gateway` (:3000)

The front door for identity and profile data.

- **Auth**: `POST /auth/register`, `POST /auth/login` (bcrypt + JWT), `GET /auth/me`.
- **Profiles** (JWT-protected): `POST /profiles`, `GET /profiles/:id`, `PATCH /profiles/:id`.
- **Health**: `GET /health` (checks DB connectivity).
- DB access: **asyncpg** with raw SQL; an idempotent DDL bootstrap at startup keeps the
  schema aligned (and widens `users.role` with `admin`). Swagger docs are FastAPI-native
  at `/docs`.
- Owns the `users` and `profiles` tables.

### 2.2 Extract Agent — `backend/agent/extract` (:3001 canonical, **:3003 on this machine**)

Turns a profile (or raw text / a crawled page) into a canonical attribute set plus an
embedding, and writes one row per user into `extracted_profiles`.

- `POST /extract/profile` — load a gateway profile by `userId`, map it to canonical
  attributes, run the pipeline, upsert the row. (This is what the app calls.)
- `POST /extract/text`, `POST /extract/crawl` — same pipeline from raw text / a URL.
- `GET /extracted/:userId` — fetch the stored extraction.
- **Extraction** uses an OpenAI-compatible chat model with a strict JSON-schema response
  (`founder.schema.js` / `investor.schema.js`) against the FPT-cloud endpoint.
- **Embedding** uses `text-embedding-3-small` (1536-dim). If `OPENAI_API_KEY` is unset, it
  falls back to a deterministic **feature-hash embedding** (L2-normalized bag-of-words) so
  cosine ranking still works on lexical overlap — the pipeline never hard-fails.
- On boot it self-initializes its schema (`db/init.js` → `schema.sql`): creates the
  `vector` extension, the `extracted_profiles` table, and an **HNSW** cosine index.

> **Port note:** `.env.example` pins `PORT=3001`, but on the dev machine Uptime Kuma
> occupies 3001, so it runs on **3003** locally. The vite proxy and nginx tunnels are
> configured for 3003 accordingly.

### 2.3 Matching Engine — `backend/matching-engine` (:3002)

Ranks the opposite side of the market for a given user.

- `GET /matches/founders/:userId/investors` and
  `GET /matches/investors/:userId/founders` — the app's two live routes. Return `404` if
  the user has no `extracted_profiles` row yet (the app then triggers extraction and
  retries).
- `GET /matches` — **proxied** to the Python matching API (`MATCHING_API_URL`, default
  `:8000`) for the composite research-grade list.
- **Algorithm** (`matching.service.js` + `scoring.js`):
  1. Pull the requester's embedding, then fetch the `CANDIDATE_POOL` (default 50) nearest
     rows of the target role by pgvector cosine distance (`<=>`), with optional
     sector/stage/region pre-filters on the JSONB attributes.
  2. Compute `vectorScore = 1 − cosine_distance`.
  3. Compute `attributeScore` from explainable rules: **sector overlap** (Jaccard, ×0.4),
     **stage match** (+0.3), **geography match / global** (+0.2), **check size fits ask**
     (+0.05–0.1). Each firing rule adds a human-readable reason.
  4. Final `score = 0.7 · vectorScore + 0.3 · attributeScore` (weights configurable via
     `MATCH_VECTOR_WEIGHT` / `MATCH_ATTR_WEIGHT`), sorted descending, sliced to `limit`.

### 2.4 AI Data Platform — `ai-data-platform/` (Python)

A deterministic supervisor (event-sourced sagas, leased jobs, retries, dead-letter) that
drives isolated `feynman` / `pi` agent runtimes over stdio JSON-RPC for enrichment,
extraction, ranking, drafting, and verification — every fact cited, every draft
fact-checked. Exposes a FastAPI **read API + dashboard** on `:8000`
(`/entities /matches /sagas /jobs /events`). Full detail lives in its own docs:
[`ai-data-platform/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ai-data-platform/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) and
[`AGENT_FLOWS.md`](../ai-data-platform/docs/AGENT_FLOWS.md).

## 3. Request flow — "show me my matches"

```
1. User completes profile      → Gateway  POST /profiles            (row in `profiles`)
2. App requests matches        → Matching GET  /matches/.../investors
3.   no extraction yet? (404)  → Extract  POST /extract/profile     (row in `extracted_profiles`)
4. App retries matches         → Matching GET  /matches/.../investors → ranked list + reasons
5. User opens a match          → outreach draft generated (client-side template)
```

The frontend encodes exactly this in `App.jsx` / `lib/api.js`: on a `404` from the matching
engine it auto-calls extraction, then retries the match request once.

## 4. Data model

All services share **one Postgres database (`dealflow`)**.

**`users`** (gateway) — credentials + `role` (`founder` | `investor` | `admin`), links to a
profile. `admin` is never accepted by `/auth/register` — it is provisioned only by a direct
DB update (`UPDATE users SET role='admin' ...`) and unlocks the AI Data Platform dashboard.

**`profiles`** (gateway) — the structured onboarding form: `company_name`,
`stage`, `industry`, `where_you_operate`, `website[]`, `description_product`, contact,
and investor economics (`avg_initial_investment`, `annual_investment_count`,
`avg_holding_period`, `year_founded`, `num_of_employees`, …). UUID PK, `underscored`,
timestamped.

**`extracted_profiles`** (extract agent) — one row per user, the matching substrate:

| Column | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `user_id` (UUID, unique) | FK-by-convention to the gateway user |
| `role` | `founder` \| `investor` |
| `source` | `profile` \| `text` \| `crawler` |
| `attributes` (JSONB) | canonical, queryable attribute set |
| `embedding_text` | the text that was embedded |
| `embedding` (`vector(1536)`) | semantic vector, **HNSW cosine index** |

The AI Data Platform adds its own `core` / `orchestration` / `control_plane` schemas
(entities, sources with `source_url`, matches, sagas, jobs, events) via
`ai-data-platform/migrations/`.

## 5. Frontend & mobile

- **Web** (`frontend/`): Vite + React 18 + GSAP. No CORS on the backends — the dev/preview
  server and production nginx both **proxy `/api/*`** by prefix (`/api/backend` → gateway,
  `/api/agents` → extract, `/api/matches` → matching engine, `/api` → gateway fallback).
  Session (`vn.session`) and a per-user profile mirror live in `localStorage`.
- **Mobile** (`mobile/`): Expo / React Native (React Navigation, Google Fonts) mirroring
  the web screens (auth, profile form, matches, match detail). Currently backed by
  `src/data/mockData.js`.

## 6. Deployment

- **Public URL**: `https://dqplus.ddns.net`.
- **VPS** (`13.250.9.139`, Ubuntu): `nginx` terminates TLS and routes by prefix to
  **autossh reverse tunnels** from the dev machine (managed by `deploy/port-forward.sh`,
  targeting `127.0.0.1`):
  - `/` → `8443` → `proxy` container (`:5173`)
  - `/api/agents/`, `/api/extract/` → `5002` → extract agent (`:3003`)
  - `/api/matches/` → `5003` → matching engine (`:3002`)
  - `/api/backend/`, `/api/` fallback → `5000` → gateway (`:3000`)
- Production build uses `VITE_API_BASE=/api` (same-origin).
- **Local dev**: `./start.sh` boots Postgres (pgvector) via docker-compose, copies
  `.env.example → .env` where missing, and launches all three Node services with tailed
  logs into `logs/`.

### 6.1 Zero-downtime web deploys

The tunnel lands on a single local port (`:5173`), and only one container can bind a
host port — so a naive `docker compose up --build web` stops the old container before
the new one binds, dropping requests. To avoid that, the `web` service is **internal-only
and scalable** (no `container_name`, no published port, `expose: 80`, IPv4 healthcheck),
and a tiny **`proxy`** service (`nginx:1.27-alpine`, config in `deploy/proxy.conf`) owns
`:5173` and load-balances across `web` replicas:

```
VPS nginx → :8443 → SSH tunnel → proxy (:5173) ─┬─ web (old replica)
                                                └─ web (new replica)
```

The proxy resolves `web` per request via Docker DNS (`resolver 127.0.0.11`, variable
`proxy_pass`), so scaled replicas are discovered automatically, and `proxy_next_upstream`
with a short `proxy_connect_timeout` retries a sibling if one is draining.

**Deploy a frontend change** with `bash deploy/rolling-deploy.sh`: build → start a 2nd
`web` replica → wait healthy → drain the old replica by container id → back to one. Always
≥1 healthy backend behind the proxy (verified `ok=800 fail=0` under an in-flight probe).

**Operational notes**

- `docker-compose.yml` pins `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` so the `vector` extension is durable
  across container recreation (an earlier apt-installed pgvector in a sibling container was
  ephemeral).
- autossh tunnels must use an **absolute** key path with `-f` and target `127.0.0.1`
  (WSL2 mirrored networking makes docker-published ports unreachable via the LAN IP). The
  VPS sshd rate-limits rapid reconnects (~1–2 min lockout).
- Container healthchecks hit `127.0.0.1`, not `localhost` — `localhost` resolves to `::1`
  and the services bind IPv4 only, which would report a healthy container as unhealthy.
- `docker compose --scale web=N` scales **down** by removing the newest replica, so the
  rolling script drains the *old* replica by id rather than scaling down.
- Change `deploy/proxy.conf` with `docker exec dqplus-proxy nginx -s reload` (zero
  downtime); recreating the `proxy` container briefly drops `:5173`.

# VietNexus — Overview

> **A smart matchmaker for Vietnam's startup world.** VietNexus helps startups find the
> right investors — and helps that startup's story reach the right partners around the
> world.
>
> Hackathon project · `dqplus-hackathon-2026` · VAIC 2026, challenge #135
> (Deal-flow Matchmaker, sponsored by NIC) · July 2026.

*This overview is written for everyone. For the technical details, see
[`ARCHITECTURE.md`](ARCHITECTURE.md) and [`TECHNOLOGY.md`](TECHNOLOGY.md).*

---

## 1. The problem

Vietnam has a fast-growing community of startups, but connecting the right people is still
slow, manual, and mostly depends on who you happen to know:

- **Founders** struggle to find investors who are actually a good fit — the right industry,
  the right stage, the right amount of money. So they reach out to everyone and get
  ignored by most.
- **Investors** get flooded with pitches that don't match what they're looking for, and
  miss great companies simply because those companies aren't in their circle.
- **The language gap** — a promising Vietnamese startup usually doesn't have a clear,
  convincing pitch in English, so international investors and partners never hear its story.

The result: good companies don't get funded, and good money doesn't reach them.

## 2. What VietNexus does

Think of VietNexus as an expert matchmaker that never sleeps. You tell it about yourself,
and it hands you a short, ranked list of the people most worth talking to — and explains
*why* each one is a good fit.

Here's the journey:

1. **Sign up and introduce yourself.** A founder or investor fills in a short profile:
   what the company does, what stage it's at, its industry, where it operates, and (for
   investors) how much they typically invest.
2. **VietNexus understands you.** Behind the scenes, an AI reads your profile and turns it
   into a clear, structured summary of who you are and what you're looking for.
3. **It finds your best matches.** VietNexus compares you against everyone on the other
   side of the market and ranks them by how well they fit — combining the *meaning* of what
   you do with concrete facts like industry, stage, and investment size.
4. **You see the reasons.** Every match comes with a plain-language explanation ("same
   industry: fintech · matches your stage: seed · invests in your region"), so you can
   trust the list instead of guessing.
5. **You reach out.** With one click you get a ready-to-send introduction message to start
   the conversation.

For deeper research, a companion system (the **AI Data Platform**) goes further: it looks
up real information about each company from the web, keeps a link to every source so nothing
is made up, and has a second AI double-check every message to remove anything that isn't
true. In short — **the right match, with the proof to back it up.**

## 3. Who it's for

| User | What they get |
|------|---------------|
| **Founders** | A short list of investors who genuinely fit their fundraising, each with a clear reason and a ready-to-send intro. |
| **Investors** | A relevant pipeline of startups worth their time, instead of endless cold pitches. |
| **Ecosystem builders** (like NIC and accelerators) | A trustworthy, transparent way to connect the whole innovation community. |

## 4. Four kinds of connections — and honest about what's ready

The app is built around four things a founder might be looking for. Only the first is fully
working today; the others are shown with an honest "coming soon" note instead of pretending
with fake results.

| You're looking for… | Status |
|---------------------|--------|
| **Investors** that fit your raise | ✅ Working today |
| Potential customers | 🔜 Coming soon |
| Partners & mentors for R&D | 🔜 Coming soon |
| Talent to join your team | 🔜 Coming soon |

## 5. What's inside

VietNexus has two parts that work together:

- **The app** — the website and mobile app that founders and investors actually use, plus
  the services behind them that handle sign-in, profiles, and matching.
- **The AI Data Platform** — a more powerful research engine that gathers real evidence
  from the web and fact-checks every introduction. (Its own overview is in
  [`ai-data-platform/docs/OVERVIEW.md`](../ai-data-platform/docs/OVERVIEW.md).)

## 6. What works today, and what's next

**Working end to end right now:**

- Sign up, sign in, and fill in your profile (on both web and mobile).
- The AI reads your profile and prepares it for matching. It works with a paid AI service
  for the best results, and also has a built-in backup so it keeps working even without
  one.
- Matching that ranks the other side of the market and explains its reasons.
- A live public website at **https://dqplus.ddns.net**.

**Still in progress:**

- The customers, partners, and talent connections don't have a working engine yet (the app
  says so honestly).
- The introduction messages in the app are built from a simple template today; the
  fully-researched, fact-checked version lives in the AI Data Platform and isn't wired into
  the app yet.

## 7. Learn more

- [`ARCHITECTURE.md`](ARCHITECTURE.md) — how the pieces fit together (technical).
- [`TECHNOLOGY.md`](TECHNOLOGY.md) — the tools we used and why (technical).
- [`ai-data-platform/docs/`](../ai-data-platform/docs/) — the research engine's own docs.

2	name: adp-rag-graphrag
3	description: ai-data-platform now has pgvector RAG + GraphRAG + dashboard graph view; activation needs migration 004 + backfill
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 7c5aee8d-22dd-403c-b0b2-a862987e863f
8	  modified: 2026-07-18T20:37:23.046Z
9	---
10	
11	`ai-data-platform` gained three capabilities (built 2026-07-19), ported/extended from
12	the fork `NghiemNgocDuc/vietname-ai-innovation`:
13	
14	- **RAG**: `spine/embedding.py` (FPT `multilingual-e5-large` **1024-dim** — NOT
15	  text-embedding-3-small, which FPT does not host; deterministic blake2b feature-hash
16	  fallback when no `OPENAI_API_KEY`), `spine/matcher/embedding_matcher.py`
17	  (pgvector KNN over `entities` + rule attribute score, optional LLM rerank), and
18	  `POST /matches/founders/{id}/investors` + `/matches/investors/{id}/founders` on the
19	  API (caller passes the profile in the request body; returns the backend matching-engine
20	  shape score/vectorScore/attributeScore/reasons + id/name/type/rationale_*).
21	- **GraphRAG**: `spine/graph.py` (BFS over `edges` with name-slug re-resolution) +
22	  `GET /entities/{id}/graph-matches`.
23	- **Semantic /discover** + dashboard graph card & UI (fit slider, toggleable sidebar).
24	
25	**Activation is NOT automatic**: `entities.embedding` starts NULL. Must (1) apply
26	`migrations/004_embeddings.sql` (auto-applied by `bootstrap.py` glob on fresh boot; a
27	running DB needs it applied), then (2) run `uv run python scripts/embed_entities.py`
28	(source `ai-data-platform/.env` first — spine reads `os.environ`, no dotenv autoload).
29	Done once on 2026-07-19: all 115 seed entities embedded in `dqplus-postgres` (:5433).
30	
31	**FPT gotchas (learned the hard way):**
32	- FPT (`mkp-api.fptcloud.com`) sits behind **Cloudflare** → the default Python-urllib
33	  User-Agent gets a 403 "error code: 1010". Every FPT call MUST send a browser UA
34	  (done in embedding.py / embedding_matcher.py / semantic.py).
35	- FPT hosts embedders `multilingual-e5-large` and `Vietnamese_Embedding` (both 1024-dim),
36	  NOT OpenAI models. Chat models: `gemma-4-31B-it`, `Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct`, `GLM-5.2`,
37	  `DeepSeek-V4-Flash`, `gpt-oss-120b`, etc. (`GET /models` to list). Column is `vector(1024)`.
38	- Corpus and query MUST use the same embedder. So the deployed `matching-api` container
39	  needs the SAME `OPENAI_API_KEY`/model as the backfill, or query vectors won't match the
40	  stored corpus. As of 2026-07-19 the running container has OLD code + no key → rebuild
41	  (`docker compose up -d --build matching-api`) + pass the key before the API serves matches.
42	
43	Until embedded, the `/matches` RAG endpoints return empty and
44	`store.py`'s best-effort embed-on-upsert logs `embed_on_upsert_failed` (harmless).
45	
46	**DB SPLIT (2026-07-19): two separate Postgres, no longer a shared DB.**
47	- `dqplus-postgres` (host **:5433**) = BACKEND DB — ONLY `users`/`profiles`/`extracted_profiles`
48	  (gateway + extract; they self-create their tables). DB_HOST=postgres internally.
49	- `dqplus-platform-postgres` (host **:5432**) = PLATFORM DB — `entities`/`edges`/`matches`/
50	  `matches_v2`/`saga_instances`/`jobs`/`events`/`artifacts`/`llm_usage`/`workers`/`apps`/
51	  `schema_migrations`. ai-data-platform DATABASE_URL → `platform-postgres:5432`.
52	- The platform NO LONGER reads `extracted_profiles` by SQL. It fetches user profiles from
53	  the extract service over HTTP: `GET {EXTRACT_SERVICE_URL}/extracted/:userId`
54	  (`EXTRACT_SERVICE_URL=http://extract:3003`), used by `_rag_match_by_user` in api/app.py.
55	- To free host :5432, the stale predecessor container `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1`
56	  was stopped. (Supersedes the old CLAUDE.md gotcha that 5433 was used because 5432 was taken.)
57	- Data was migrated via `pg_dump` of the platform tables from the old shared DB into
58	  platform-postgres. NOTE: zsh does NOT word-split unquoted `$VAR`, so inline pg_dump `-t`
59	  flags (a `-t a -t b` variable becomes one bad pattern → "no matching tables were found").
60	
61	**Cutover done (2026-07-19): matching-engine RETIRED, platform serves /matches.**
62	- `matching-engine` is disabled in docker-compose (`profiles: ["disabled"]`), container removed.
63	- Platform now has GET `/matches/founders/{user_id}/investors` + `/matches/investors/{user_id}/founders`
64	  that SELF-FETCH the requester's profile from `extracted_profiles` by gateway user UUID
65	  (`embedding_text` + `attributes`), embed with e5-1024, RAG over the entity corpus. Founders
66	  route defaults to ALL partner types (investor/corporation/university/research_institution).
67	  Still 404s when no extracted_profiles row, preserving the app's extract-then-retry flow.
68	  (POST body-based variants also still exist.)
69	- **Public routing gotcha**: the VPS nginx (13.250.9.139) routes each `/api/*` prefix to a
70	  DEDICATED reverse-SSH tunnel, NOT through the web container's nginx. `/api/matches` → VPS
71	  port 5003. So `frontend/nginx.conf` (baked into `web`) does NOT govern the public /matches
72	  path. The fix was to repoint the `5003` tunnel in `deploy/port-forward.sh` from `:3002`
73	  (dead matching-engine) to `:8000` (platform). Editing `frontend/nginx.conf` only affects
74	  the local `:5173` proxy chain, not the public URL.
75	
76	**Spindle upgrade (2026-07-19, branch `worktree-spindle-upgrade`): module paths moved.**
77	ai-data-platform was upgraded to the deal-flow-matchmaker spindle architecture (generic
78	`spindle/` platform core + `apps/matchmaker/` plugin, North Star offline harness,
79	selfimprove P1/P2). The RAG/GraphRAG modules above now live at
80	`apps/matchmaker/{embedding,graph}.py` and `apps/matchmaker/matcher/{embedding_matcher,semantic}.py`
81	(old `spine/…` paths are gone; spine keeps only config/ids/pools/telemetry/transport +
82	supervisor shim). Cleanup saga is declared in `apps/matchmaker/app.yaml` (audit→purge)
83	with handlers in `plugin.py`. Supervisor entrypoint: `python -m spindle.app.supervisor`.
84	
85	Related: [[vietnexus-project]].
86

SKILL.md
evals

---
name: pitch-deck-creator
description: |
  Generate presentation-ready pitch decks: a structured markdown deck spec (headlines,
  bullets, visual suggestions, speaker notes) plus a Slidev slides.md that can be
  presented in the browser or exported to HTML/PPTX/PDF. Use this skill whenever the
  user wants to create a pitch deck, investor deck, sales deck, demo-day slides,
  startup presentation, partnership pitch, or any persuasive slide deck — even if they
  just say "I need slides for X" or "help me pitch Y" without using the word "deck".
---

# Pitch Deck Creator

## Overview

Creates structured pitch decks following proven frameworks for investor pitches, sales
presentations, or internal strategy decks. Produces two artifacts:

1. **`deck-spec.md`** — the thinking document: per-slide headlines, bullets, visual
   suggestions, and speaker notes. This is where the narrative gets built and reviewed.
2. **`slides.md`** — a presentation-ready [Slidev](https://sli.dev) deck generated from
   the spec. Runs in the browser with `npx @slidev/cli`, exports to PPTX/PDF/HTML.

Build the spec first, then render it to Slidev. The spec is where persuasion lives;
the Slidev file is just its faithful rendering. Don't skip the spec and write slides
directly — decks written slide-first tend to become lists of facts instead of a story.

## Workflow

### Step 1: Define the Deck

Establish these four things before writing anything:

1. **Purpose**: Investor raise, sales pitch, internal strategy, or partnership?
2. **Audience**: Who are you presenting to? (VCs think in markets and returns; a CTO
   thinks in risk and integration; a demo-day crowd thinks in memorability.)

# VietNexus — Project Guide for the Business Team

*A plain-language guide: what we're building, how far the technology is, and how to pitch it.*

**Live site: https://dqplus.ddns.net** · VAIC Hackathon 2026 · Challenge #135 "Deal-flow Matchmaker" (sponsored by NIC) · July 2026

---

## 1. What is VietNexus, in one paragraph

VietNexus is a **smart matchmaker for Vietnam's startup ecosystem**. A founder or an investor fills in one short profile, and the system hands back a short, ranked list of the people most worth talking to — investors, corporations, universities, research institutions — with a plain-language explanation of *why* each one fits ("same industry: fintech · matches your stage: seed · invests in your region"). Think of it as an expert deal-flow analyst that never sleeps and always shows its reasoning.

## 2. The problem we solve

Vietnam's startup community is growing fast, but connections still happen through personal networks:

- **Founders** can't tell which investors actually fit their industry, stage, and check size — so they email everyone and get ignored by most.
- **Investors** drown in pitches that don't match their thesis, and miss great companies outside their circle.
- **The language gap** means promising Vietnamese startups rarely have a clear English story, so international money and partners never hear about them.

The result: good companies don't get funded, and good money doesn't reach them. VietNexus replaces "who you happen to know" with "who actually fits you — with proof."

## 3. How it works (no technical background needed)

The user journey has five steps:

1. **Sign up and introduce yourself.** A short profile form: what the company does, its stage and industry, where it operates, and (for investors) typical investment size.
2. **The AI reads your profile.** It turns your free-text answers into a clean, structured summary of who you are and what you're looking for — the same way a good analyst would take notes.
3. **It searches the whole market for you.** The system compares the *meaning* of your profile against everyone on the other side (not just keyword matching — "food delivery app" and "restaurant logistics platform" are understood as related), then layers on hard facts: industry overlap, stage fit, geography, whether the check size fits the ask.
4. **Every match comes with reasons.** No black box. Each result says exactly why it ranked where it did, so users can trust the list.
5. **One click to reach out.** The app drafts an introduction message to start the conversation.

Behind the app sits a second, more powerful engine — the **AI Data Platform** — that researches companies from real web sources, keeps a citation for every fact so nothing is invented, maps *relationships* between players in the ecosystem (who invested in whom, who partners with whom), and fact-checks drafts with a second AI before they go out. Its motto: **the right match, with the proof to back it up.**

## 4. How much of the technology is actually built

Honest status, as of July 19, 2026. This is a working product with a live public website — not just slides.

### ✅ Working end-to-end today

| Capability | What it means |
|---|---|
| **Live public website** | https://dqplus.ddns.net — anyone can sign up and use it right now. |
| **Accounts & profiles** | Register, log in, fill in the founder or investor profile. Real data, no mock-ups. |
| **AI profile understanding** | The AI reads each profile and prepares it for matching, using a Vietnamese-capable multilingual AI model (via FPT Cloud). It even has a built-in backup mode so the product keeps working if the paid AI service is unavailable. |
| **Semantic matching with explanations** | Founders get ranked matches across **four partner types** — investors, corporations, universities, research institutions — each with a score and plain-language reasons. Investors get a ranked startup pipeline. |
| **Filters** | Users can filter matches by partner type and by sector (multi-select). |
| **Ecosystem knowledge base** | A seeded corpus of ~115 real Vietnamese ecosystem entities (investors, corporations, universities…) that matches are drawn from, plus a relationship graph connecting them. |
| **Relationship-aware matching (GraphRAG)** | Beyond "you two are similar," the system can walk the ecosystem's relationship network — e.g., surfacing an investor because they backed companies connected to your space. |
| **Admin dashboard** | An internal dashboard (admin-only login) to inspect entities, matches, jobs, and an interactive graph view of the ecosystem. |
| **Professional-grade infrastructure** | The whole stack runs in containers with zero-downtime deployments — we can ship updates while the site stays up. Automated end-to-end tests cover the core flows. |

### 🟡 Built, but not yet connected into the app

| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| **Fact-checked outreach drafts** | The AI Data Platform can research a company from the web, cite every source, draft an intro, and have a second AI verify it. Today the app itself still uses a simple template for drafts — wiring the researched version into the app is the next step. |
| **Mobile app** | The mobile screens (sign-in, profile, matches) exist and mirror the web app, but currently show sample data rather than live matches. |

### 🔜 Not built yet (and the app says so honestly)

| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| **"Find customers" and "find talent" matching** | The app shows these tabs with an honest "coming soon" notice instead of faking results. Matching for investors, partners/mentors, and institutions is live; customer and talent matching would reuse the same engine with new data. |

**A one-line summary you can use:** *the core promise — profile in, explained matches out — is fully built and live on the public internet; the research-and-evidence layer exists and works, and connecting it into the everyday user flow is what's next.*

## 5. What makes it defensible (why this isn't "just ChatGPT")

- **Explainability.** Every match shows its reasons. Trust is the product.
- **Evidence, not hallucination.** The research engine keeps a link to the source of every fact, and a second AI fact-checks drafts before they're shown.
- **A relationship graph of the ecosystem**, not just a list — matching can follow real-world connections (investments, partnerships), which generic AI tools can't do.
- **Built for Vietnam**: Vietnamese-capable AI models, seeded with real Vietnamese ecosystem data, aligned with NIC's mission of connecting the innovation community.
- **Honest by design**: no fake data anywhere in the product — unfinished features say "coming soon" rather than pretending.

## 6. Pitch toolkit

### 30-second elevator pitch

> "In Vietnam, fundraising still runs on personal networks — founders cold-email a hundred investors and hear back from none, while investors drown in pitches that don't fit their thesis. **VietNexus** fixes both sides: fill in one profile, and our AI ranks the whole ecosystem for you — investors, corporates, universities — and *explains why* each match fits. It's live today at dqplus.ddns.net, matching against real Vietnamese ecosystem data, with every claim backed by a cited source. The right match, with the proof to back it up."

### 3-minute narrative (demo-day structure: problem → solution → demo → traction → ask)

1. **Problem** — Deal-flow in Vietnam is manual and network-bound. Good companies go unfunded; good money sits idle. (Make it personal: "ask any founder how many cold emails they sent last year.")
2. **Solution** — An AI matchmaker that understands the *meaning* of what you do, ranks the other side of the market, and explains every match in plain language. Four partner types, not just investors.
3. **Demo** — Do it live (it's a real site): sign up → fill the profile → watch ranked matches appear with reasons → filter by sector → open a match. If asked "how", show the admin graph view of the ecosystem.
4. **Traction / what's real** — Live public product; ~115 real ecosystem entities seeded; matching, filters, and the relationship graph all working; evidence-cited research engine built and running. Built in weeks by a small team.
5. **The ask** — (Tailor to the audience: hackathon judges → "this is a working foundation for NIC's national deal-flow platform"; partners → data partnerships to grow the entity corpus; investors → pilot with an accelerator batch.)

### Answers to the questions she'll get asked

- **"Is this real or a prototype?"** — Real and public: https://dqplus.ddns.net. Sign up yourself. The core flow (profile → AI analysis → explained matches) is fully live.
- **"How is this different from a directory or LinkedIn?"** — Directories make you search; VietNexus *ranks the whole market for you* and explains why. And it understands meaning, not just keywords.
- **"Can we trust what the AI says?"** — That's our core design principle: every match shows its reasons, and the research engine cites a source for every fact and fact-checks drafts with a second AI.
- **"What if the AI service goes down or is too expensive?"** — The product has a built-in fallback matching mode, so it degrades gracefully instead of breaking.
- **"What's not done?"** — Customer/talent matching tabs (honestly labeled "coming soon"), the mobile app runs on sample data, and the researched outreach drafts aren't wired into the app yet. We say this proactively — honesty is part of the brand.
- **"How does it make money?"** *(if asked)* — Natural options: freemium for founders, subscription for investors/accelerators wanting curated pipeline, and ecosystem licensing (NIC, universities, corporate innovation teams). Not committed yet — this was built as a hackathon product first.

## 7. Small glossary (the only jargon worth knowing)

- **AI matching / "semantic" matching** — matching by *meaning*, not keywords. "Aquaculture IoT" and "smart shrimp farming" count as close.
- **RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)** — the AI answers using a real database of facts we control, instead of its imagination. This is what keeps answers grounded.
- **GraphRAG** — the same idea, but also using the *relationship map* (who invested in whom, who partners with whom) to find non-obvious matches.
- **Entity** — any player in the ecosystem: an investor, corporation, university, or research institution in our knowledge base.
- **AI Data Platform** — our back-office research engine: gathers evidence from the web, cites sources, fact-checks drafts.

---

*Questions? The technical docs live in the project repository (`docs/OVERVIEW.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`), and the team can demo any part of this live.*

**A one-line summary you can use:** *the core promise — profile in, explained matches out — is fully built and live on the public internet; the research-and-evidence layer exists and works, and connecting it into the everyday user flow is what's next.*

## 5. What makes it defensible (why this isn't "just ChatGPT")

- **Explainability.** Every match shows its reasons. Trust is the product.
- **Evidence, not hallucination.** The research engine keeps a link to the source of every fact, and a second AI fact-checks drafts before they're shown.
- **A relationship graph of the ecosystem**, not just a list — matching can follow real-world connections (investments, partnerships), which generic AI tools can't do.
- **Built for Vietnam**: Vietnamese-capable AI models, seeded with real Vietnamese ecosystem data, aligned with NIC's mission of connecting the innovation community.
- **Honest by design**: no fake data anywhere in the product — unfinished features say "coming soon" rather than pretending.

## 6. Pitch toolkit

### 30-second elevator pitch

> "In Vietnam, fundraising still runs on personal networks — founders cold-email a hundred investors and hear back from none, while investors drown in pitches that don't fit their thesis. **VietNexus** fixes both sides: fill in one profile, and our AI ranks the whole ecosystem for you — investors, corporates, universities — and *explains why* each match fits. It's live today at dqplus.ddns.net, matching against real Vietnamese ecosystem data, with every claim backed by a cited source. The right match, with the proof to back it up."

### 3-minute narrative (demo-day structure: problem → solution → demo → traction → ask)

1. **Problem** — Deal-flow in Vietnam is manual and network-bound. Good companies go unfunded; good money sits idle. (Make it personal: "ask any founder how many cold emails they sent last year.")
2. **Solution** — An AI matchmaker that understands the *meaning* of what you do, ranks the other side of the market, and explains every match in plain language. Four partner types, not just investors.
3. **Demo** — Do it live (it's a real site): sign up → fill the profile → watch ranked matches appear with reasons → filter by sector → open a match. If asked "how", show the admin graph view of the ecosystem.
4. **Traction / what's real** — Live public product; ~115 real ecosystem entities seeded; matching, filters, and the relationship graph all working; evidence-cited research engine built and running. Built in weeks by a small team.
5. **The ask** — (Tailor to the audience: hackathon judges → "this is a working foundation for NIC's national deal-flow platform"; partners → data partnerships to grow the entity corpus; investors → pilot with an accelerator batch.)

### Answers to the questions she'll get asked

- **"Is this real or a prototype?"** — Real and public: https://dqplus.ddns.net. Sign up yourself. The core flow (profile → AI analysis → explained matches) is fully live.
- **"How is this different from a directory or LinkedIn?"** — Directories make you search; VietNexus *ranks the whole market for you* and explains why. And it understands meaning, not just keywords.
- **"Can we trust what the AI says?"** — That's our core design principle: every match shows its reasons, and the research engine cites a source for every fact and fact-checks drafts with a second AI.
- **"What if the AI service goes down or is too expensive?"** — The product has a built-in fallback matching mode, so it degrades gracefully instead of breaking.
- **"What's not done?"** — Customer/talent matching tabs (honestly labeled "coming soon"), the mobile app runs on sample data, and the researched outreach drafts aren't wired into the app yet. We say this proactively — honesty is part of the brand.
- **"How does it make money?"** *(if asked)* — Natural options: freemium for founders, subscription for investors/accelerators wanting curated pipeline, and ecosystem licensing (NIC, universities, corporate innovation teams). Not committed yet — this was built as a hackathon product first.

## 7. Small glossary (the only jargon worth knowing)

- **AI matching / "semantic" matching** — matching by *meaning*, not keywords. "Aquaculture IoT" and "smart shrimp farming" count as close.
- **RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)** — the AI answers using a real database of facts we control, instead of its imagination. This is what keeps answers grounded.
- **GraphRAG** — the same idea, but also using the *relationship map* (who invested in whom, who partners with whom) to find non-obvious matches.
- **Entity** — any player in the ecosystem: an investor, corporation, university, or research institution in our knowledge base.
- **AI Data Platform** — our back-office research engine: gathers evidence from the web, cites sources, fact-checks drafts.

---

*Questions? The technical docs live in the project repository (`docs/OVERVIEW.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`), and the team can demo any part of this live.*

Think of it as an expert deal-flow analyst that never sleeps and always shows its reasoning.

## 2\. The problem we solve

Vietnam's startup community is growing fast, but connections still happen through personal networks:

  

  - **Founders** can't tell which investors actually fit their industry, stage, and check size — so they email everyone and get ignored by most.
  - **Investors** drown in pitches that don't match their thesis, and miss great companies outside their circle.
  - **The language gap** means promising Vietnamese startups rarely have a clear English story, so international money and partners never hear about them.

  

The result: good companies don't get funded, and good money doesn't reach them. VietNexus replaces "who you happen to know" with "who actually fits you — with proof."

## 3\. How it works (no technical background needed)

The user journey has five steps:

  

1.  **Sign up and introduce yourself.** A short profile form: what the company does, its stage and industry, where it operates, and (for investors) typical investment size.
2.  **The AI reads your profile.** It turns your free-text answers into a clean, structured summary of who you are and what you're looking for — the same way a good analyst would take notes.
3.  **It searches the whole market for you.** The system compares the *meaning* of your profile against everyone on the other side (not just keyword matching — "food delivery app" and "restaurant logistics platform" are understood as related), then layers on hard facts: industry overlap, stage fit, geography, whether the check size fits the ask.
4.  **Every match comes with reasons.** No black box. Each result says exactly why it ranked where it did, so users can trust the list.
5.  **One click to reach out.** The app drafts an introduction message to start the conversation.

**A one-line summary you can use:** *the core promise — profile in, explained matches out — is fully built and live on the public internet; the research-and-evidence layer exists and works, and connecting it into the everyday user flow is what's next.*

## 5\. What makes it defensible (why this isn't "just ChatGPT")

  - **Explainability.** Every match shows its reasons. Trust is the product.
  - **Evidence, not hallucination.** The research engine keeps a link to the source of every fact, and a second AI fact-checks drafts before they're shown.
  - **A relationship graph of the ecosystem**, not just a list — matching can follow real-world connections (investments, partnerships), which generic AI tools can't do.
  - **Built for Vietnam**: Vietnamese-capable AI models, seeded with real Vietnamese ecosystem data, aligned with NIC's mission of connecting the innovation community.
  - **Honest by design**: no fake data anywhere in the product — unfinished features say "coming soon" rather than pretending.

## 6\. Pitch toolkit

### 30-second elevator pitch

"In Vietnam, fundraising still runs on personal networks — founders cold-email a hundred investors and hear back from none, while investors drown in pitches that don't fit their thesis. **VietNexus** fixes both sides: fill in one profile, and our AI ranks the whole ecosystem for you — investors, corporates, universities — and *explains why* each match fits. It's live today at dqplus.ddns.net, matching against real Vietnamese ecosystem data, with every claim backed by a cited source. The right match, with the proof to back it up."

### 3-minute narrative (demo-day structure: problem → solution → demo → traction → ask)

1.  **Problem** — Deal-flow in Vietnam is manual and network-bound. Good companies go unfunded; good money sits idle. (Make it personal: "ask any founder how many cold emails they sent last year.")
2.  **Solution** — An AI matchmaker that understands the *meaning* of what you do, ranks the other side of the market, and explains every match in plain language. Four partner types, not just investors.
3.  **Demo** — Do it live (it's a real site): sign up → fill the profile → watch ranked matches appear with reasons → filter by sector → open a match. If asked "how", show the admin graph view of the ecosystem.
4.  **Traction / what's real** — Live public product; \~115 real ecosystem entities seeded; matching, filters, and the relationship graph all working; evidence-cited research engine built and running. Built in weeks by a small team.
5.  **The ask** — (Tailor to the audience: hackathon judges → "this is a working foundation for NIC's national deal-flow platform"; partners → data partnerships to grow the entity corpus; investors → pilot with an accelerator batch.)

### Answers to the questions she'll get asked

  - **"Is this real or a prototype?"** — Real and public: <https://dqplus.ddns.net>. Sign up yourself. The core flow (profile → AI analysis → explained matches) is fully live.
  - **"How is this different from a directory or LinkedIn?"** — Directories make you search; VietNexus *ranks the whole market for you* and explains why. And it understands meaning, not just keywords.
  - **"Can we trust what the AI says?"** — That's our core design principle: every match shows its reasons, and the research engine cites a source for every fact and fact-checks drafts with a second AI.
  - **"What if the AI service goes down or is too expensive?"** — The product has a built-in fallback matching mode, so it degrades gracefully instead of breaking.
  - **"What's not done?"** — Customer/talent matching tabs (honestly labeled "coming soon"), the mobile app runs on sample data, and the researched outreach drafts aren't wired into the app yet. We say this proactively — honesty is part of the brand.
  - **"How does it make money?"** *(if asked)* — Natural options: freemium for founders, subscription for investors/accelerators wanting curated pipeline, and ecosystem licensing (NIC, universities, corporate innovation teams). Not committed yet — this was built as a hackathon product first.

## 7\. Small glossary (the only jargon worth knowing)

  - **AI matching / "semantic" matching** — matching by *meaning*, not keywords. "Aquaculture IoT" and "smart shrimp farming" count as close.
  - **RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)** — the AI answers using a real database of facts we control, instead of its imagination. This is what keeps answers grounded.
  - **GraphRAG** — the same idea, but also using the *relationship map* (who invested in whom, who partners with whom) to find non-obvious matches.
  - **Entity** — any player in the ecosystem: an investor, corporation, university, or research institution in our knowledge base.
  - **AI Data Platform** — our back-office research engine: gathers evidence from the web, cites sources, fact-checks drafts.

  

  

*Questions? The technical docs live in the project repository (**docs/OVERVIEW.md**,* *docs/ARCHITECTURE.md**), and the team can demo any part of this live
